sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Barbara, a 31-year-old female, presents with a one-week history of abdominal pain.
She reports that the pain has been gradually increasing in severity.
She also reports nausea and vomiting, with an estimated three instances of nausea and a couple of instances of vomiting in the past week.
She attributes her abdominal pain to increased coffee consumption, specifically Starbucks nitrocolbrus, and has been drinking a lot of coffee recently, including a venti-sized cup.
She also reports that her abdominal pain worsens with physical activity, such as jogging or running.
She denies fever and reports that her diabetes has been under control, with a blood sugar level of 800.
She has a history of diabetes type 2 and asthma.
She has been taking metformin 500mg twice a day and flovent twice a day with albuterol as needed.
Past medical history: diabetes type 2, asthma.
Review of systems: nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain.
Current medications: metformin 1000mg in the morning and 500mg in the evening, flovent twice a day, albuterol as needed.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's heart exam reveals a grade three out of six systolic ejection murmur that is unchanged from prior exam.
The abdominal exam reveals mild tenderness in the epigastrium of the abdomen, with no tenderness in the right lower quadrant or right upper quadrant.
The lung exam reveals mild end-expiratory wheezes, but otherwise normal lung exam with excellent air movement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I diagnose Barbara with acute gastritis, likely related to increased caffeine intake and possibly exacerbated by her history of heavy alcohol use.
Problem list: Acute gastritis; diabetes type 2; asthma.
Plan:
I prescribed zantac 150mg twice a day to help with acid in the stomach and pain.
I also recommend reducing her caffeine intake, keeping it to less than two or three drinks a week, and cutting back on spicy food.
I adjusted her metformin dose to 1000mg in the morning and 500mg in the evening.
I ordered a urinalysis, urine pregnancy test, CBC, and comprehensive metabolic panel to rule out any other underlying conditions.
I will see her back in four weeks.
Follow-up:
I will see Barbara back in four weeks.
